rss.title: Women can wait years for an endometriosis diagnosis. New tech could change that
rss.snippet: A new scan technique could spot areas of endometriosis missed by conventional scans, scientists say.

rss.title: Claimants in Johnson & Johnson talcum powder case rise to 7,000
rss.snippet: The case, which opened in the High Court on Wednesday, originally involved 3,000 claimants and is set to become the largest product liability case in UK history.

arxiv_papers.title: Global stability of a Hebbian/anti-Hebbian network for principal subspace learning
arxiv_papers.snippet: Biological neural networks self-organize according to local synaptic modifications to produce stable computations. How modifications at the synaptic level give rise to such computations at the network level remains an open question. Pehlevan et al. [Neur. Comp. 27 (2015), 1461--1495] proposed a mode

arxiv_papers.title: On graphical domination for threshold-linear networks with recurrent excitation and global inhibition
arxiv_papers.snippet: Graphical domination was first introduced in [1] in the context of combinatorial threshold-linear networks (CTLNs). There it was shown that when a domination relationship exists between a pair of vertices in a graph, certain fixed points in the corresponding CTLN can be ruled out. Here we prove two

podcast: [Male Roles, Obligations and Options for Building a Fulfilling Life | Scott Galloway] Scott Galloway is a professor of marketing at New York University's Stern School of Business. We discuss masculine roles and obligations of past, present and future. We explore which are timeless and which are changing, and positive steps boys and men can take to create meaning and stability in their lives. We cover work, finances, health and relationships to build a grounded, purposeful life. We
